summary: Steganographic code execution from logo.png on require()
details: vite-plugin-logo's index.js, when required, walks up to 5 parent directories searching for public/assets/logo.png. If the file contains the marker '__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__', the bytes after the marker are base64-decoded and executed via `new Function('require', code)(require)`, giving the embedded payload full Node privileges (filesystem, network, child_process). The decode+exec block is wrapped in a bare `try { } catch (e) {}` so any failure (no planted PNG, bad base64, payload exception) is silent, and the loader uses single-letter variables and a generic cache-marker name to disguise the pathway. The package's advertised purpose (a vite plugin to optimize/serve logo assets) is a cover — the file contains no plugin logic, only the stego loader. A malicious logo file planted in any consuming project (or shipped by a companion package) becomes arbitrary code execution on every developer/CI machine that loads this plugin.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
